text: Distance is the total length of the actual path travelled by an object. It does not depend on direction and is always a positive quantity.
Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final position of an object in a particular direction. It can be positive, negative, or zero.
Scalar quantity (only magnitude)
Vector quantity (magnitude + direction)
Total path length travelled
Final position - Initial position

A person walks 3 m east and then 4 m west.
Distance = 3 + 4 = 7 m
Displacement = 3 - 4 = -1 m (1 m towards west)

A boy walks 5 m north, then 5 m south.
Find:
1) Distance
2) Displacement
Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time. It tells how fast an object is moving but does not give direction.
Velocity is the displacement of an object per unit time. It tells both how fast and in which direction an object is moving.
Scalar quantity (magnitude only)
Vector quantity (magnitude + direction)
Speed = Distance / Time
Velocity = Displacement / Time

A car travels 100 m east in 10 s.
Speed = 100 / 10 = 10 m/s
Velocity = 100 / 10 = 10 m/s east

A runner completes one full circular track of radius 14 m in 28 s.
Find:
1) Speed
2) Velocity
Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with respect to time. It tells how quickly velocity changes.
Uniform acceleration means velocity changes by equal amounts in equal intervals of time.
Non-uniform acceleration means velocity changes by unequal amounts in equal intervals of time.
Acceleration (a) = (Final velocity - Initial velocity) / Time
Acceleration is a vector quantity (has magnitude and direction).

A car increases its velocity from 10 m/s to 30 m/s in 5 s.
a = (30 - 10) / 5 = 20 / 5 = 4 m/s²

A bike slows down from 20 m/s to 5 m/s in 3 s.
Find the acceleration.
v = u + at
Use when time, initial velocity and acceleration are known.
Use when displacement, time, initial velocity and acceleration are involved.
Use when time is not given.

A body starts from rest (u = 0) and accelerates at 2 m/s² for 5 s.
Using v = u + at:
v = 0 + (2)(5) = 10 m/s

A car moves with initial velocity 5 m/s and acceleration 3 m/s² for 4 s.
Find final velocity and displacement.
In a position-time (x-t) graph, slope represents velocity.

1. Straight line → constant velocity
2. Horizontal line → object at rest
3. Curve → changing velocity
In a velocity-time (v-t) graph, slope represents acceleration and area under the graph represents displacement.

1. Straight line → constant acceleration
2. Horizontal line → constant velocity
3. Area under graph = displacement
In an acceleration-time (a-t) graph, area under the graph represents change in velocity.

1. Horizontal line → constant acceleration
2. Area under graph = change in velocity
If the v-t graph is a straight line passing through origin, what does it represent?
